key_insight: Treat neural-network depth adaptation as an optimal-control problem, then use a posteriori error estimates to place new layers where approximation error is largest.
engineering_relevance: This gives a principled way to refine surrogate models for PDE and scientific computing workflows, improving accuracy without uniformly increasing network size.
method: Formulate training as a continuous-time optimal control problem with layer-wise weights and biases.
Use dual weighted residual error estimation to decompose total error into interval-wise contributions.
Insert new layers at locations of maximum estimated error to adapt depth efficiently.
evidence_signal: The abstract reports tests on scientific datasets, including learning the observable-to-parameter map for Navier–Stokes, with consistently better generalization than existing adaptation methods.
watch_item: The approach relies on computable error estimators and continuous-to-discrete assumptions, so implementation cost and estimator robustness may be challenging for large or noisy problems.
paper_title: An optimal control approach for neural network architecture adaptation with a posteriori error estimation
